chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Reward of Visiting the Prophet {saws} and the Imams {a.s}, Peace Be Upon Them All

text: اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى يَبْدَأُ بِالنَّظَرِ إِلَى زُوَّارِ قَبْرِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَشِيَّةَ عَرَفَةَ » قِيلَ لَهُ قَبْلَ نَظَرِهِ إِلَى أَهْلِ اَلْمَوْقِفِ قَالَ «نَعَمْ» قِيلَ لَهُ وَ كَيْفَ ذَاكَ قَالَ «لِأَنَّ فِي أُولَئِكَ أَوْلاَدَ زِنًى وَ لَيْسَ فِي هَؤُلاَءِ أَوْلاَدُ زِنًى.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3171 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, begins by looking at the visitors of the grave of Imam Hussain ibn Ali (as) on the eve of Arafah."
It was asked: "Does He look at them before looking at the people at the [Arafah] gathering?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
It was then asked: "How is that?"
Imam (as) said: "Because among those [at Arafah] are illegitimate children, and among these [visitors of Husayn's grave], there are no illegitimate children."
